title: Head of Chemical Trading
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced and entrepreneurial Head of Chemical Trading to lead proprietary trading activities for a range of liquid chemical products. This role offers full ownership of the trading book and requires a deep understanding of physical and financial markets.

Responsibilities

  • Lead proprietary trading activities for liquid chemical products including LPG, olefins, polypropylene, and downstream derivatives to drive profitable business growth with full P&L accountability
  • Develop and execute independent trading strategies leveraging physical and financial instruments to optimize trading performance and market positioning
  • Originate, source, and market physical cargoes by leveraging industry networks to increase trading volumes and expand market presence
  • Analyze global market fundamentals, price trends, geopolitical developments, and supply-demand dynamics to make timely, data-driven trading decisions
  • Manage trading positions and market exposure while ensuring compliance with company policies and risk management frameworks to mitigate financial risks
  • Design and implement hedging and arbitrage strategies using derivatives such as futures, swaps, and options to optimize portfolio risk and returns
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with operations, shipping, finance, and risk management teams to ensure seamless trade execution, logistics coordination, and settlement processes
  • Identify new product opportunities along the chemical value chain and establish long-term partnerships with customers, suppliers, and brokers to drive sustainable business growth
  • Prepare and present regular trading performance reports and market analyses to support strategic and operational decision-making by senior management
  • Undertake ad-hoc projects or tasks assigned by management to support business objectives and continuous improvement

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Experience in Asian and/or Chinese chemical markets, with familiarity in Asia-related commodity futures/OTC markets and logistics to enhance regional trading effectiveness
  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Chemistry, Business, Trading, Finance, or a related field to provide foundational knowledge supporting trading activities
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in proprietary or physical trading of chemicals, energy, or related commodities demonstrating proven trading expertise
  • Established industry network with demonstrated ability to originate, develop, and scale trading business independently to drive commercial success
  • Strong understanding of chemical market fundamentals, physical supply chains, pricing mechanisms including derivative pricing models, and risk management practices to inform trading strategies
  • Commercially driven and entrepreneurial mindset capable of thriving in a lean, performance-oriented trading environment
  • Strong analytical, numerical, and negotiation skills to perform effectively under pressure and secure advantageous trading positions

postedCompany.description:
At GreenLane Talent Acquisition, we redefine the recruitment experience for both clients and job seekers. As a leading talent acquisition partner, we specialize in connecting top-tier professionals with leading companies. Our comprehensive approach covers everything from precise position identification to seamless onboarding, ensuring a perfect fit for both employers and candidates.


For job seekers, GreenLane is more than a recruitment agency; we are your career advocate. Our team is dedicated to understanding your unique skills, aspirations, and preferences, guiding you towards opportunities that align with your career goals. We believe in the transformative power of exceptional talent, and we are here to support you in achieving your professional ambitions.


Whether you are an employer seeking top talent or a job seeker looking for the next career move, GreenLane Talent Acquisition is your trusted partner on the journey to success.
postedCompany.name: GREENLANE PRIVATE LTD.
